title: Implement Policy-Based Authorization for ShopFlow
description: Create custom authorization policies with requirements and handlers for ShopFlow e-commerce platform. Implement age-based, permission-based, and combined policy authorization.
instructions:
Build a policy-based authorization system for ShopFlow that goes beyond simple roles. Policies allow flexible, reusable authorization rules.

**Requirements:**

1. **Configure Policies in Program.cs:**
   - Create a policy that requires users to be 18+ years old (AgeRequirement)
   - Create a policy that requires 'CanEditProducts' permission (PermissionRequirement)
   - Create a policy that requires both Admin role AND 18+ age (CombinedRequirement)
   - Create a policy for premium customers (PremiumCustomerRequirement)

2. **Implement Custom Authorization Handlers:**
   - AgeAuthorizationHandler: Checks claim 'Age' >= 18
   - PermissionAuthorizationHandler: Checks if user has specific permission claim
   - PremiumCustomerHandler: Checks if user has 'IsPremium' claim with value 'true'

3. **Create Policy-Protected Endpoints:**
   - GET /api/products/restricted - Requires 'Age18Plus' policy
   - POST /api/products - Requires 'CanEditProducts' policy
   - GET /api/admin/advanced - Requires 'AdminAndAdult' policy (Admin role + 18+)
   - GET /api/customer/premium-content - Requires 'PremiumCustomer' policy
   - DELETE /api/products/{id} - Requires 'CanDeleteProducts' policy

4. **Register Handlers and Policies:**
   - AddSingleton for each authorization handler
   - Use AddAuthorization with policy definitions

**Scenarios to Support:**
- Regular users under 18 cannot access age-restricted products (alcohol, mature content)
- Only users with explicit 'CanEditProducts' permission can create/modify products
- Premium content only accessible to customers with IsPremium claim
- Advanced admin operations require both admin role AND verified adult status

**Hint:** Use IAuthorizationService for imperative checks inside handlers and policy-based attributes for declarative protection.

testCases.description: Should configure AddAuthorization with custom policies

hints.text: Define policies in AddAuthorization(options => { options.AddPolicy("PolicyName", policy => policy.RequireClaim(...)); })
Create custom requirements as classes implementing IAuthorizationRequirement.
Implement handlers by inheriting from AuthorizationHandler<TRequirement> and override HandleRequirementAsync.
Use context.Succeed(requirement) to indicate the requirement is met, or do nothing to fail.

commonMistakes.mistake: Not registering the authorization handlers in DI
commonMistakes.consequence: Handlers won't be discovered and policies will fail silently or throw errors.
The requirement never succeeds, causing authorization to always fail.
commonMistakes.correction: Call context.Succeed(requirement) when your condition is met. Don't call Fail() - just don't succeed.

commonMistakes.consequence: Runtime error: 'Policy XXX was not found' when the endpoint is accessed.
commonMistakes.correction: Define all policies in AddAuthorization during service configuration before building the app.

commonMistakes.mistake: Checking claims without validating their format
commonMistakes.consequence: Claims like 'Age' might not parse correctly, causing logic errors.
commonMistakes.correction: Always validate and parse claims safely. Use int.TryParse for numeric claims.
